prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-PlNDkg.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.

parameters.task: Add user notification preferences: create preferences model, preferences service, integrate with notification service, add tests

7. All files in tests/ - test patterns used
8. The tsconfig.json and package.json for build/test configuration

Read all the files completely and report back their full contents. Be very thorough - I need to see every file to understand the architecture before making changes.

alternatives.option: Make PreferencesService a required constructor parameter of NotificationService
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would break all existing code that constructs NotificationService without preferences. The setter approach is backward-compatible.

alternatives.option: Check preferences inside event handlers instead of dispatch()
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would require duplicating preference checks in every handler. Centralizing in dispatch() catches all notification types including future ones.

alternatives.option: Opt-in model where users must create preferences to receive any notifications
alternatives.reason_rejected: Poor UX — new users would miss notifications until preferences are created. Opt-out (default send-all) is safer and more common.

assumptions: Email is currently the only notification channel — preference checks are scoped to email in dispatch()
userId in metadata is the correct identifier for looking up preferences
Future channels (SMS, webhook) will need their own preference check branches in dispatch()
parameters.context: Need to add user notification preferences so users can control email notification settings (enabled/disabled, frequency, category subscriptions). Must integrate with existing event-driven notification system without breaking backward compatibility.
parameters.rationale: PreferencesService is a separate service injected into NotificationService via setPreferencesService(). When not set, all notifications are sent (backward-compatible). When set, dispatch() checks shouldSendEmail() before sending. This avoids a hard constructor dependency, keeps the notification service testable without preferences, and follows the existing dependency injection pattern. Preferences are stored via PreferencesRepository following the existing repository pattern. Default preferences send all categories at instant frequency — users opt out rather than opt in.
parameters.summary: User notification preferences use opt-in setter on NotificationService with fallback to send-all when no preferences exist

parameters.detail:
Completed:
1. PREFERENCES MODEL (src/models/user-preferences.ts): EmailFrequency type (instant/daily/weekly/never), EmailNotificationSettings interface (enabled, frequency, subscribedCategories), UserPreferences interface, Create/Update input types, ALL_NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES constant, DEFAULT_EMAIL_SETTINGS constant.
2. PREFERENCES REPOSITORY (src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts): Extends BaseRepository with findByUserId() method. Stores email settings as JSON in the database.
3. PREFERENCES SERVICE (src/services/preferences.service.ts): CRUD for user preferences, shouldSendEmail() method for preference checking, getAvailableCategories(). Default behavior: send all notifications (opt-out model).
4. NOTIFICATION SERVICE INTEGRATION (src/services/notification.service.ts): Added setPreferencesService() setter, dispatch() now checks preferences before sending email notifications. Backward-compatible — works without preferences service.
5. EXPORTS (src/index.ts): All new types, interfaces, constants, repository, and service exported.
6. TESTS (tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts): 27 new tests covering CRUD, shouldSendEmail logic, and notification service integration with preferences.
7. All 116 tests pass (89 existing + 27 new). TypeScript compiles clean.

Key files:
- NEW: src/models/user-preferences.ts
- NEW: src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts
- NEW: src/services/preferences.service.ts
- NEW: tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts
- MODIFIED: src/services/notification.service.ts (preference check in dispatch)
- MODIFIED: src/index.ts (new exports)
parameters.summary: Agent 3 complete: User notification preferences implemented with model, repository, service, notification integration, and 27 tests
